name: avenger
shortDescription: In Egyptian mythology, the concept of 'avenger' isn't embodied by a single deity but is rather a role fulfilled by various gods, particularly Horus, in his quest to avenge his father Osiris. This act of vengeance is crucial for maintaining cosmic order and ensuring justice.
description: The role of 'avenger' in Egyptian mythology is primarily associated with Horus, who sought to avenge the murder of his father, Osiris, by his uncle Set. This act of vengeance wasn't simply a personal vendetta; it was essential for restoring Ma'at (cosmic order) and ensuring the continuity of kingship. The conflict between Horus and Set is a central theme in Egyptian mythology, representing the struggle between order and chaos. Through a series of trials and battles, Horus ultimately defeats Set, reclaiming his rightful place as pharaoh and avenging his father's death. This victory symbolizes the triumph of good over evil and the restoration of balance to the world. While no single deity is named 'Avenger', the concept of seeking justice and retribution is a recurring motif, often embodied by Horus and other deities who uphold Ma'at. The successful avenging of Osiris allows for his resurrection and reign in the underworld, further emphasizing the importance of vengeance in the cycle of life, death, and rebirth. The role of avenger also highlights the importance of filial duty and the responsibility of descendants to uphold justice and avenge wrongs done to their ancestors.

sources.title: The Oxford Guide to Egyptian Mythology
sources.author: Donald B. Redford
